summary: Tests cover main requirements (glob patterns, label assignment, priority/conflict resolution, CLI, workflow validation) but lack comprehensive edge case handling. Unit tests are well-organized but don't validate rule structure thoroughly (e.g., invalid priority types, missing fields in rules). Integration tests are creative but depend on fixture files not shown in the code, making full assessment impossible. Design is reasonable with helper functions and pytest fixtures, but hard-coded expected values in act tests are not verified, and the workflow file itself isn't provided for validation. Would catch regressions in happy paths but likely miss subtle bugs from malformed inputs or complex edge cases.
